title: "unknown device, wubi install to harddrive"

### Post by ubuntumaybe on 2011-07-11
I am getting the grub rescue prompt and I cannot get beyond it. The error is unknown device then a string of numbers and letters. I had originally installed to windows using wubi then I installed to harddrive. I have included my grub.cfg. THanks for your help with this.

#
# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE
#
# It is automatically generated by grub-mkconfig using templates
# from /etc/grub.d and settings from /etc/default/grub
#

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/00_header ###
if [ -s $prefix/grubenv ]; then
  set have_grubenv=true
  load_env
fi
set default="0"
if [ "${prev_saved_entry}" ]; then
  set saved_entry="${prev_saved_entry}"
  save_env saved_entry
  set prev_saved_entry=
  save_env prev_saved_entry
  set boot_once=true
fi

function savedefault {
  if [ -z "${boot_once}" ]; then
    saved_entry="${chosen}"
    save_env saved_entry
  fi
}

function recordfail {
  set recordfail=1
  if [ -n "${have_grubenv}" ]; then if [ -z "${boot_once}" ]; then save_env recordfail; fi; fi
}

function load_video {
}

if [ "${recordfail}" = 1 ]; then
  set timeout=-1
else
  set timeout=10
fi
### END /etc/grub.d/00_header ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###
set menu_color_normal=white/black
set menu_color_highlight=black/light-gray
### END /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-22-generic' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos6)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 67c51e26-8f04-4a42-82cb-951abe33fd21
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-22-generic root=UUID=67c51e26-8f04-4a42-82cb-951abe33fd21 ro   quiet splash
	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-22-generic
}
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-22-generic (recovery mode)' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos6)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 67c51e26-8f04-4a42-82cb-951abe33fd21
	echo	'Loading Linux 2.6.35-22-generic ...'
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-22-generic root=UUID=67c51e26-8f04-4a42-82cb-951abe33fd21 ro single 
	echo	'Loading initial ramdisk ...'
	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-22-generic
}
### END /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/20_linux_xen ###
### END /etc/grub.d/20_linux_xen ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/20_memtest86+ ###
menuentry "Memory test (memtest86+)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos6)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 67c51e26-8f04-4a42-82cb-951abe33fd21
	linux16	/boot/memtest86+.bin
}
menuentry "Memory test (memtest86+, serial console 115200)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos6)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 67c51e26-8f04-4a42-82cb-951abe33fd21
	linux16	/boot/memtest86+.bin console=ttyS0,115200n8
}
### END /etc/grub.d/20_memtest86+ ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###
menuentry "Ubuntu (on /dev/sda1)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ntfs
	set root='(hd0,msdos1)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 7a645d96645d564b
	drivemap -s (hd0) ${root}
	chainloader +1
}
menuentry "Microsoft Windows XP Home Edition (on /dev/sdb1)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ntfs
	set root='(hd1,msdos1)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 8e6c28836c2867e3
	drivemap -s (hd0) ${root}
	chainloader +1
}
menuentry "linux (on /dev/sdb6)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd1,msdos6)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set aa8cad3c-b02f-4db9-bf6f-a2290ffd9bca
	linux /boot/vmlinuz BOOT_IMAGE=linux root=LABEL=PCLinuxOS noapic nolapic acpi=off resume=LABEL=linuxswap
	initrd (hd0,5)/boot/initrd.img
}
menuentry "failsafe (on /dev/sdb6)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd1,msdos6)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set aa8cad3c-b02f-4db9-bf6f-a2290ffd9bca
	linux /boot/vmlinuz BOOT_IMAGE=failsafe root=LABEL=PCLinuxOS noapic nolapic failsafe acpi=off
	initrd (hd0,5)/boot/initrd.img
}
menuentry "MEPIS ISO at hda1 (on /dev/sdb6)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd1,msdos6)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set aa8cad3c-b02f-4db9-bf6f-a2290ffd9bca
	linux /boot/vmlinuz init=/etc/init apm=power-off vga=791
	initrd /boot/initrd.gz
}
### END /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/40_custom ###
# This file provides an easy way to add custom menu entries.  Simply type the
# menu entries you want to add after this comment.  Be careful not to change
# the 'exec tail' line above.
### END /etc/grub.d/40_custom ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/41_custom ###
if [ -f  $prefix/custom.cfg ]; then
  source $prefix/custom.cfg;
fi
### END /etc/grub.d/41_custom ###

### Post by sanderd17 on 2011-07-11
First: please put that file in CODE tags (click on the # symbol in edit mode)

Second: Run the boot info script from a live CD: [http://bootinfoscript.sourceforge.net/](http://bootinfoscript.sourceforge.net/) (and place it in CODE tags)

This will give us more info.

It is probably solvable by reinstalling GRUB as shown here: [https://help.ubuntu.com/community/RecoveringUbuntuAfterInstallingWindows#Recovery%20Using%20the%20Ubuntu%20Desktop/Live%20CD%20%28RECOMMENDED%29](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/RecoveringUbuntuAfterInstallingWindows#Recovery%20Using%20the%20Ubuntu%20Desktop/Live%20CD%20%28RECOMMENDED%29)

But I would want to see the output of the boot info script before you do that.

---
